title: Netherlands Sole Proprietorship Registration (Eenmanszaak, KVK Form 1)
description: Register a new one-person sole proprietorship (eenmanszaak) — or report that an existing business or establishment is being taken over and continued as an eenmanszaak — in the Business Register (Handelsregister), on behalf of the Netherlands Chamber of Commerce (Kamer van Koophandel, KVK). This is the Dutch analogue to sg/acra/sole-proprietorship-registration, gb/companies-house/company-incorporation-in01, ie/cro/company-incorporation, ca/on/registration/business-incorporation, and de/gewerbeamt/business-registration, and is the first schema in this registry for the Netherlands (NL), a new jurisdiction. An eenmanszaak has exactly one owner, even if other people work in the business; the owner may only have one eenmanszaak at a time, though it may operate more than one establishment. Scoped to a single individual owner completing KVK Form 1 (Formulier 1): partnerships (vennootschap onder firma, two or more owners, a distinct KVK form), corporate or nominee position holders, and the equivalent kvk.nl/eenmanszaakregistration online pre-fill path are out of scope — see VERIFICATION.md. KVK requires the applicant to visit a KVK office in person, with a valid proof of identity, to complete the registration even when this form has been filled in and printed in advance; this document does not submit the registration or book that appointment, and does not model appointment scheduling. The official form is bilingual: an English translation (this document's primary source) auto-fills an authoritative Dutch original that alone is signed and submitted. The live kvk.nl and Business Register sources are always authoritative.

source.documentRef: KVK Form 1 (Formulier 1) — 'Register an eenmanszaak (sole proprietorship)', English translation of version August 2025 (a bilingual EN/NL fillable AcroForm PDF; pages 1-5 are the English translation, pages 6-10 are the identically-fielded authoritative Dutch original)

verification.notes: Source-derived reference schema authored directly from KVK Form 1's own AcroForm field structure, extracted programmatically with pdfjs-dist (getAnnotations()/getTextContent() across both the English pages 1-5 and the mirrored Dutch pages 6-10, confirming the two page sets share identical field names and checkbox export values). This is the same PDF-field-extraction rigor as ca/on/registration/business-incorporation and de/gewerbeamt/business-registration, stronger than the automated-fetch-and-summarize sourcing used for sg/acra/sole-proprietorship-registration (Bizfile has no downloadable form at all). Programmatically checked every annotation's `required` flag: none of KVK Form 1's fields set the PDF's own Required bit, so this document's `required: true` assignments reflect the form's own prose instructions and standard registration necessity (core identity, trade name, activity, address, and signature-block fields), not a confirmed submission-time gate — the same honesty discipline applied to au/ato/individual-tax-return-mytax and sg/iras/individual-income-tax-return-formb1. Status remains 'draft'; no live-portal or in-person walkthrough was performed. See VERIFICATION.md for the field-by-field source table and out-of-scope items.

fields.name: registrationType
fields.label: Are you registering a new eenmanszaak, or continuing an existing business or establishment as an eenmanszaak?
fields.description: Determines which start-date and prior-business fields apply. 'Continuing' means taking over an existing business or establishment (e.g. by purchase) and carrying it on as a sole proprietorship.
fields.sourceRef: Form 1, §1.1: Are you registering a new eenmanszaak or continuing an existing business or establishment?

fields.name: startDate
fields.label: Start date of the eenmanszaak
fields.description: The date the applicant started setting up the new business — usually the date they start helping customers, though earlier registration is sometimes necessary (e.g. to arrange contracts).
fields.sourceRef: Form 1, §1.1: 'I am starting a new eenmanszaak. Start date of the eenmanszaak:'

fields.name: existingBusinessName
fields.label: Name of the existing business or establishment being taken over
fields.description: The registered name of the business or establishment the applicant is taking over and continuing as an eenmanszaak.
fields.sourceRef: Form 1, §1.1.1: 'The name of the business or establishment is:'

fields.name: existingBusinessLocation
fields.label: Location of the existing business or establishment being taken over
fields.description: The registered location (place/municipality) of the business or establishment being taken over.
fields.sourceRef: Form 1, §1.1.1: 'The location of the business or establishment is:'

fields.name: existingBusinessKvkNumber
fields.label: KVK number of the existing business or establishment
fields.description: The Business Register (KVK) number already assigned to the business or establishment being taken over.

fields.name: continuationDate
fields.label: Date of continuation of the business or establishment
fields.description: The date the applicant takes over the business or establishment — the date on the signed takeover contract.
fields.sourceRef: Form 1, §1.1.2: 'The date of continuation of the business or establishment is:'

fields.name: originalEstablishmentDate
fields.label: Original start date of the business or establishment in the Business Register
fields.description: The original 'establishment date' the business or establishment first received in the Business Register. If unknown, KVK enters it instead, so this field is never required even on the continuation path.
fields.sourceRef: Form 1, §1.1.3: 'The start date of the company or establishment in the Business Register is... If you do not know the date, KVK will enter it for you.'

fields.name: primaryTradeName
fields.label: Trade name
fields.description: The trade name (handelsnaam) the eenmanszaak uses to do business, written first if there is more than one.

fields.name: hasAdditionalTradeNames
fields.label: Does the eenmanszaak have more than one trade name?
fields.description: An eenmanszaak may use more than one trade name, for example when it carries out different activities under different names.
fields.sourceRef: Form 1, §2.1: 'Does the eenmanszaak have 1 or more trade names?'

fields.name: additionalTradeNames
fields.label: Additional trade names
fields.description: Any further trade names beyond the primary one, most important first.

fields.name: businessActivities
fields.label: Activities of the eenmanszaak
fields.description: Description of the eenmanszaak's current activities, products, and services — not planned future activities. KVK uses this description to assign one or more SBI (Standard Industrial Classification) codes, so it must be described clearly. Must be written in Dutch, per the form's own instructions.
fields.sourceRef: Form 1, §2.2: 'What are the activities of the eenmanszaak?' — 'Please note: You must describe your activities in Dutch.'

fields.name: mainBusinessActivity
fields.label: Main activity of the eenmanszaak
fields.description: Which of the described activities above is the eenmanszaak's main activity.
fields.sourceRef: Form 1, §2.3: 'What is the main activity of the eenmanszaak?'

fields.name: sellsToConsumersRetail
fields.label: Does the eenmanszaak sell products to consumers (retail)?
fields.sourceRef: Form 1, §2.4: 'Does the eenmanszaak sell products to consumers (retail)?'

fields.name: retailOtherChannel
fields.label: Sells via another channel not listed

fields.name: retailOtherChannelDescription
fields.label: Description of the other sales channel

fields.name: sellsWholesale
fields.label: Does the eenmanszaak sell products to other businesses or legal entities (wholesale)?
fields.sourceRef: Form 1, §2.5: 'Does the eenmanszaak sell products to other businesses or legal entities (wholesale)?'

fields.name: importsGoods
fields.label: Does the eenmanszaak import products from abroad?
fields.description: Includes importing ingredients or components for an end product, not only finished goods for resale.
fields.sourceRef: Form 1, §2.6: 'Does the eenmanszaak import products from abroad?'

fields.name: exportsGoods
fields.label: Does the eenmanszaak export products abroad?
fields.sourceRef: Form 1, §2.7: 'Does the eenmanszaak export products abroad?'

fields.name: businessVisitingAddress
fields.label: Visiting address of the eenmanszaak
fields.description: The address of the eenmanszaak's principal establishment. Cannot be a PO Box. If the visiting or postal address is not the owner's home address, KVK may require proof (e.g. rental agreement, proof of ownership, or a declaration of consent) that the eenmanszaak is allowed to use it.
fields.sourceRef: Form 1, §2.8: 'What is the visiting address of the eenmanszaak?'

fields.name: hasDifferentPostalAddress
fields.label: Is the postal address different from the visiting address?
fields.sourceRef: Form 1, §2.9: 'Is the postal address of the eenmanszaak different from the visiting address?'

fields.name: businessPostalAddress
fields.label: Postal address of the eenmanszaak
fields.description: May be a PO Box address, unlike the visiting address.
fields.sourceRef: Form 1, §2.9: 'Yes, the postal address or PO Box address is:'

fields.name: shieldVisitingAddress
fields.label: Shield the visiting address from the public Business Register?
fields.description: If shielded, the visiting address is no longer visible in the public Business Register. Shielding requires a postal address different from the visiting address.
fields.sourceRef: Form 1, §2.10: 'Do you want to shield the visiting address, so that the address is not visible in the Business Register?'

fields.name: businessPhoneNumber1
fields.label: Telephone number 1
fields.description: A contact telephone number for the eenmanszaak. Not published in the Business Register.

fields.name: businessPhoneNumber2
fields.label: Telephone number 2
fields.description: An optional second contact telephone number for the eenmanszaak.

fields.name: businessWebsiteUrl
fields.label: Website (URL)
fields.description: The eenmanszaak's website address, if any. Published in the Business Register.

fields.name: businessEmailAddress
fields.label: Business email address
fields.description: The eenmanszaak's email address. Published in the Business Register.

fields.name: employeeCount15PlusHoursPerWeek
fields.label: Number of people who work 15 hours or more per week
fields.description: Includes the owner, employees, hired and temporary workers, and other persons (e.g. family members) who work in the business. If the eenmanszaak has more than one establishment, count only the principal establishment.
fields.sourceRef: Form 1, §2.12: 'Number of people who work 15 hours or more per week:'

fields.name: employeeCountUnder15HoursPerWeek
fields.label: Number of people who work less than 15 hours per week
fields.description: Same population as employeeCount15PlusHoursPerWeek, counted separately for those working under 15 hours per week at the principal establishment.
fields.sourceRef: Form 1, §2.12: 'Number of people who work less than 15 hours per week:'

fields.name: ownerFirstNames
fields.label: Owner — first name(s)
fields.description: As shown in the owner's passport.
fields.sourceRef: Form 1, §3.1: 'First name(s) (as shown in your passport):'

fields.name: ownerCitizenServiceNumber
fields.label: Owner — citizen service number (BSN)
fields.description: The Dutch Citizen Service Number (Burgerservicenummer, BSN), found on a Dutch passport, ID card, or driving licence, or issued on municipal registration for those from another country. Modelled as an 8-or-9-digit string per the BSN's publicly documented format (not independently confirmed against KVK's own field validation, which sets no length constraint on this field); see VERIFICATION.md.

fields.name: allowMarketingUseOfAddress
fields.label: May other businesses use the visiting and postal address for marketing (unsolicited advertising)?
fields.description: If declined, KVK records on the Business Register extract that other businesses may not use the eenmanszaak's visiting or postal address for marketing purposes, reducing unsolicited advertising mail and doorstep sellers.
fields.sourceRef: Form 1, §4.1: 'Are sellers allowed to use the visiting and postal address of the eenmanszaak from the Business Register?'

fields.name: hiresOutEmployeesForPayment
fields.label: Does the eenmanszaak sometimes hire out employees to other businesses or legal entities for payment?
fields.description: Triggers a notification obligation under the Waadi Act (Wet allocatie arbeidskrachten door intermediairs). Answer 'No' if hiring out employees is itself one of the eenmanszaak's activities (e.g. a temp agency) — that case is described under businessActivities instead, not this field.
fields.sourceRef: Form 1, §5.1: 'Does the eenmanszaak sometimes hire out employees to businesses or legal entities for payment?'

fields.name: signerLastNameAndInitials
fields.label: Signer — last name and initial(s)
fields.description: By signing, the signer declares that all information entered is correct and that they are authorised to sign on behalf of the eenmanszaak.

documents.label: Owner's valid proof of identity
documents.sourceRef: Form 1, §6: 'Signing this form' — 'KVK requires a copy of a valid proof of identity from the owner of the eenmanszaak'; accepted types: Passport, Dutch driving licence, European identity card, A Dutch foreign nationals document

documents.label: Owner's handwritten signature
documents.statement: The owner of the eenmanszaak must sign the form with a handwritten signature (not a copy or scan), on both the English and Dutch copies if both were used to complete the form; only the signed Dutch copy is given to KVK.
documents.sourceRef: Form 1, §3.1 and §6.1: handwritten signature requirements; 'About the English version of this form'

steps.title: Details of the owner of the eenmanszaak
